div.h2: Syscall Timing: What You Actually Measure vs True Cost

g.text: Single getpid() Trace — One Entry/Exit Pair
What the Statistics Mean (and Don't Mean)

div.p:
The Observer Effect Law:
elapsed_ns = actual_syscall_time + 2 × ptrace_context_switch_cost
For fast syscalls: ptrace cost >> actual cost → numbers are misleading
For slow syscalls (I/O waits): actual cost >> ptrace cost → numbers are accurate
Use counts and ratios. Distrust absolute times for fast syscalls.

g.text: T2: ptrace(PTRACE_SYSCALL) → context switch to tracee

text.tspan: if read() takes 10× more than write(),
read() blocked 500 ms on disk I/O
absolute ns values for fast syscalls
